title: Prepare and access the Tip (A-Axis) PCA for replacement
summary: Safely prepare for Tip (A-Axis) PCA replacement by gathering the specified replacement part, PPE, and tools, locking out/tagging out the tipper, removing the station side panel if needed, and using Figure 7-18 to identify the Tip PCA area to be serviced.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when preparing to access the Tip (A-Axis) controller PCA area for replacement work as described in section 7.3.17 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to perform the full PCA replacement beyond the initial access/removal steps, because the supplied source content is truncated.
Do not use this procedure without first performing lockout/tagout of the tipper.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, remove the station side panel if needed, and identify the Tip PCA area using the referenced figure.

safety_notes: Perform lockout/tagout of the tipper before proceeding.
Do not proceed without performing LOTO.

system_preconditions: Tipper is available to be locked out/tagged out before panel removal and PCA access.
external_references: Operator Station Lockout/Tagout on page 2 of the manual

visual_references.description: Figure 7-18 Tip PCA maintenance diagram used to identify the Tip (A-Axis) PCA location during access/removal.

steps.title: Gather required replacement part, PPE, and tools
steps.purpose: Ensure all required items are available before beginning lockout and access work.
steps.instruction: Gather the replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and wire ties before starting the procedure.
steps.expected_result: All listed parts, PPE, and tools are available at the work area.
steps.healthy_condition: The replacement Controller PCA and all listed PPE and tools are present and ready for use.
steps.failure_condition: One or more required items are missing or unavailable.

actions.label: Verify required items
actions.exact_action: Gather the required items: replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and wire ties.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the figure context for the Tip PCA procedure while preparing the correct replacement part and service setup.

stop_or_escalate_if: The replacement Controller PCA is not available.

source_evidence.text: Gather the required items: replacement Controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, 1.5-mm hex wrench, 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and wire ties.

Nearby procedure text states required tools and materials include a replacement controller PCA, safety glasses, gloves, a 1.5-mm hex wrench, a 3/8-in. wrench or socket, and wire ties, with an estimated time of 15 minutes.

steps.title: Lock out and tag out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe state before removing panels or accessing internal components.
steps.instruction: Perform lockout/tagout of the tipper using the referenced Operator Station Lockout/Tagout procedure on page 2 before continuing.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out/tagged out and safe for access work.
steps.healthy_condition: LOTO has been completed before any panel removal or internal access.
steps.failure_condition: LOTO has not been completed or cannot be verified.

actions.label: Perform LOTO
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.

stop_or_escalate_if: The equipment cannot be verified as locked out/tagged out.
Any condition prevents safe isolation of the tipper.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
Do not proceed without performing LOTO.
The removal procedure begins with LOTO of the tipper.

steps.title: Remove side panel fasteners if needed
steps.purpose: Release the station side panel so the internal motor and PCA area can be accessed.
steps.instruction: If needed to gain access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.
steps.expected_result: The side panel fasteners are removed and the panel is ready to be taken off.
steps.healthy_condition: All nine self-tapping fasteners are removed without preventing panel removal.
steps.failure_condition: The fasteners cannot be removed as described or the panel remains secured.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the Tip PCA maintenance diagram as the visual support referenced for the access/removal procedure.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed as described.
The fasteners cannot be removed with the specified tool.
Any unexpected obstruction prevents safe panel access.

source_evidence.text: If necessary, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.
If the side panel cannot be removed as described, stop and escalate to qualified site support.
The removal procedure begins with LOTO of the tipper and may require removing nine self-tapping fasteners from the side panel to access internal components.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel
steps.purpose: Open access to the motor and Tip (A-Axis) PCA area.
steps.instruction: Remove the station side panel to access the motor area after the panel fasteners have been removed if required.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed and the motor area is accessible.
steps.healthy_condition: The side panel is off and the motor area is exposed for further PCA access work.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed or the motor area remains inaccessible.

actions.label: Remove side panel
actions.exact_action: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look at the figure for the Tip PCA location and the accessed area after side panel removal.

stop_or_escalate_if: The side panel cannot be removed as described.
The motor area is not accessible after panel removal.
Any unexpected condition is found after opening the panel.

source_evidence.text: Remove the station side panel to access the motor.
If the side panel cannot be removed as described, stop and escalate to qualified site support.
This maintenance diagram is relevant to replacing the controller PCA for the tipper or A-axis assembly.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

steps.title: Identify the Tip PCA using Figure 7-18
steps.purpose: Confirm the correct PCA area has been accessed before continuing with replacement work.
steps.instruction: Use Figure 7-18 to identify the Tip PCA or controller PCA in the accessed area.
steps.expected_result: The Tip PCA/controller PCA is visually identified in the opened service area.
steps.healthy_condition: The technician can identify the Tip PCA location using Figure 7-18.
steps.failure_condition: The Tip PCA/controller PCA cannot be confidently identified from the accessed area and figure reference.

actions.label: Identify Tip PCA
actions.exact_action: Use Figure 7-18 to identify the Tip PCA / controller PCA in the accessed area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Identify the Tip PCA / controller PCA location shown in Figure 7-18.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tip PCA cannot be identified with confidence.
The accessed area does not match the referenced figure.
Further replacement steps are needed but not present in the supplied source.

source_evidence.text: Use Figure 7-18 to identify the Tip PCA / controller PCA in the accessed area.
Figure 7-18 is referenced as visual support for PCA identification, but no unsupported visual details were invented.
Figure 7-18 titled "Tip PCA" appears on page 173 of the OptiSweep Operation and Maintenance Manual in section 7.3.17, Tip (or A-Axis) PCA Replacement, specifically near section 7.3.17.1 Removal.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.

success_criteria: Required replacement part, PPE, and tools are gathered.
The tipper has been locked out/tagged out.
The station side panel has been removed if needed.
The motor and Tip (A-Axis) PCA area are accessible.
The Tip PCA/controller PCA has been identified using Figure 7-18.
healthy_conditions: Safety glasses and gloves are available and used.
LOTO is completed before access work begins.
Nine self-tapping fasteners can be removed if side panel access is required.
The station side panel can be removed to access the motor.
Figure 7-18 supports identification of the Tip PCA area.
failure_conditions: LOTO is not performed before proceeding.
Required tools, PPE, or replacement part are missing.
The side panel cannot be removed as described.
The Tip PCA/controller PCA cannot be identified from the accessed area.
The source content does not provide the remaining replacement steps.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate to qualified site support if the side panel cannot be removed as described.
Do not proceed if LOTO cannot be completed or verified.
Escalate for SME review if additional replacement steps are needed beyond this access/removal portion because the supplied source is truncated.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Figure 7-18 Tip PCA location and controller PCA identification in the accessed service area.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-18 appears on page 173 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_17_1_removal.
